commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b486def151e9461bc9b46821d38420783656a81d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch reshapes the official external plugin catalog as a feed document and adds a guarded hosted loader with an in-memory last-known-good snapshot fallback plus focused tests.

PR surface: Source +451, Tests +380, Other +5. Total +836 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the failing path is the focused invalid-snapshot test fixture, which seeds a snapshot under a URL the loader will not read. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR is draft, protected by the `maintainer` label, lacks real behavior proof, and needs a contributor or maintainer fix for the snapshot test fixture before normal merge review can continue.

target.title: Add hosted catalog snapshot fallback
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feeds-stack-context:start -->
## Hosted feeds stack context

This PR is PR3 in the hosted feeds implementation stack. The stack implements the model proposed in [RFC 19](https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/19).

Review order:
- [RFC 19](https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/19): hosted feeds for plugins and skills
- [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) PR1: feed-shaped bundled fallback
- [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) PR2: guarded hosted fetch
- [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877) PR3: last-known-good snapshot fallback
- [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964) PR4: persistent SQLite snapshots
- [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969) PR5: local source profile validation
- [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981) PR6: OpenClaw config profiles

This PR's role: Adds an in-memory last-known-good snapshot path so transient hosted feed failures can keep using the most recent accepted feed.

Stack boundaries: the current stack is catalog plumbing only. It does not auto-install packages, does not switch installer registries, does not execute feed/source auth, and does not enforce signed feed envelopes yet. Those are later slices after Vince and the maintainers are happy with the core fallback and profile model.
<!-- hosted-feeds-stack-context:end -->

## Journey Context

This is step 3 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. The stack is [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846) -> [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868) -> [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877). #95846 made the bundled catalog feed-shaped. #95868 added guarded hosted fetch. This PR adds the last-known-good snapshot contract that makes the hosted path safe when the register endpoint is unchanged or temporarily unhealthy.

## What This PR Does

- Adds a narrow `HostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogSnapshotStore` interface.
- Adds an in-memory snapshot store for tests and future runtime binding.
- Writes the exact hosted response body plus hosted metadata and `savedAt` after a successful hosted load.
- Uses a cached snapshot on 304, non-OK hosted responses, invalid hosted JSON/schema, or fetch failures.
- Validates cached snapshot body checksum before use.
- Validates cached snapshot schema before use.
- Preserves `expectedSha256` pinning for snapshots, so a stale cached body cannot satisfy a caller-pinned checksum it does not match.
- Falls back to the bundled catalog when no valid snapshot exists.

## What This PR Intentionally Does Not Do

This PR does not choose a production persistence location, wire SQLite/runtime storage, enable the hosted loader by default, add source profiles, add auth, add signed envelopes, add regional feeds, or change search/install behavior. It defines the loader-level snapshot semantics that a later runtime-owned persistent store can bind to.

## How To Review

Review this as fallback semantics, not as the final storage implementation. The key behavior is: hosted feed first, validated last-known-good snapshot second, bundled catalog last. A snapshot write failure is intentionally non-fatal because a good hosted response should still be usable even if cache persistence fails.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (27 tests)
- `timeout 420s pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --uncommitted` initially found a real snapshot checksum-pinning issue; this PR fixes it. A later top-stack `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base refs/remotes/origin/main` found the hosted-fetch lazy import issue in #95868; that is also fixed and covered. Final top-stack review reported no discrete actionable correctness issues.
